comments.author: @stephencolebrook8602
comments.text: This is all about a tourist visit. There's no expression of the intense stress that is truly Bahamian. No mention of the third world existence we suffer because of intense corruption and being invade by Haiti from within because of their strategic positioning in govt departments we stupidly allowed them to blossom, not unlike most countries are experiencing. So the Bahamas you see today or of yesteryear will be 100% Caribbean at this rate. . . . by the way we're not in the 'Caribbean Sea', we're in the Atlantic Ocean 30 minute from Miami by flight/

comments.author: @TopUnrealPlanet4K
comments.text: This video is honestly phenomenal. The storytelling feels so effortless and immersive that you completely lose yourself in the atmosphere. Every shot feels meaningful, every transition feels intentional, and the emotional tone quietly builds in a way that stays with you long after the video ends. It’s rare to see content crafted with this much care and artistic restraint. After watching, I found myself wanting to continue exploring that same sense of mystery, and we recently shared a journey on our channel that uncovers even more unexpected layers in a similar world.

It means a lot to know that the care and artistic restraint came through. I'll definitely check out your channel for more explorations!

comments.author: @philliproberts8388
comments.text: This video only shows the capital New Providence and our second city Grand Bahama Freeport. It does not show any of our family islands and their communities.  Also we do have the nation University of The Bahamas where you can obtain up to masters degree. We are a proud and dynamic nation who love all of our visitors and most importantly Our God. Thanks  for high lighting our great nation and God bless.

comments.author: @SouthernArcher1962
comments.text: This will upset many people, but I just want to give my opinion. Nassau Bahamas is one of so many ports around the world that caters to Cruise ships. The thing I don't understand is why so many people book passage on a Cruise ship to visit the Tourist area of another country when you barely visit the Tourist parts of your own country? I was able to visit 11 different countries while serving in the Navy from 80 to 92. As much as possible, I tried to get away from the Tourist places and find out of the way spots that only the natives know about. I also lived in the Philippines for two years. What most people don't know is, all of these places have a totally different vibe when the ships are gone, than they do during tourist season. I have visited Trinidad in the Caribbean, from the moments following leaving the Airport, I went as far away from there as I could get until it was time to leave. I do understand that being on a Cruise ship, you get to see a few places during the cruise and the activities of the ship. I had rather take a trip to Key west then a boat to one of the smaller islands, away from the tourist spots. Just my opinion. No, I have never been on a 5 Star Cruise ship. Two Naval Ships, an Oiler replenish ship USS ROANOKE,and a Carrier CVN-70. Yes, much different I know. I had rather go on adventures like Cody and Kellie go on. They are on YouTube and FB.

replies.author: @jaimestewart8295
replies.text: We also stayed at a five star resort in Aruba one time and it was the most awesome time I've ever had if you've never done it then you should try it , I didn't want to come home 😂

replies.author: @jaimestewart8295
replies.text: People go just to have fun me and my wife went there on a cruise and had a blast the food was great the drinking was fun the bars on the ship was ok , kinda crowded for me but it was just a wonderful life experience for us that's why people go
